content.problemStatement:
You are given two **binary** arrays AAA and BBB, of lengths NNN and MMM, respectively.

You can perform the following operation:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§min‚Å°(‚à£A‚à£,M)1 \leq i \leq \min(|A|, M)1‚â§i‚â§min(‚à£A‚à£,M)) such that Ai=BiA_i = B_iAi‚Äã=Bi‚Äã.

Here, ‚à£A‚à£|A|‚à£A‚à£ denotes the current length of AAA.
Delete AiA_iAi‚Äã from AAA. This reduces the length of AAA by 111.
Then, re-index the remaining elements of AAA to start from 111, without changing their order.
For example, suppose A=[0,0,0,1]A = [0, 0, 0, 1]A=[0,0,0,1] and B=[1,0]B = [1, 0]B=[1,0].


Choosing i=2i = 2i=2 (valid because A2=B2=0A_2 = B_2 = 0A2‚Äã=B2‚Äã=0) allows us to delete A2=0A_2 = 0A2‚Äã=0 from AAA.

After this, A=[0,0,1]A = [0, 0, 1]A=[0,0,1].
Since the elements were re-indexed, we can once again choose i=2i = 2i=2 to delete A2A_2A2‚Äã, obtaining A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1]. No more operations can be performed.
Find the **minimum** possible final length of AAA, if you perform operations optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚Äî the lengths of AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible final length of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, A=[0,0,0,1]A = [0, 0, 0, 1]A=[0,0,0,1] and B=[1,0]B = [1, 0]B=[1,0].

Choose i=2i = 2i=2, valid because A2=B2=0A_2 = B_2 = 0A2‚Äã=B2‚Äã=0.
After deletion, A=[0,0,1]A = [0, 0, 1]A=[0,0,1].
Choose i=2i = 2i=2 again, valid because A2=B2=0A_2 = B_2 = 0A2‚Äã=B2‚Äã=0.
After deletion, A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1].

This is the best we can do.
Test case 222: No moves can be made at all.
Test case 333: One optimal sequence is:

Choose i=1i = 1i=1, since A1=B1=1A_1 = B_1 = 1A1‚Äã=B1‚Äã=1. After the deletion, A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1].
Choose i=2i = 2i=2, since A2=B2=1A_2 = B_2 = 1A2‚Äã=B2‚Äã=1 now. After the deletion, A=[0]A = [0]A=[0].
No more moves can be made.
